name: Red Snapper a La Mexicaine
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Finely chopped fresh herbs (oregano, thyme & chervil)
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the fish filets with salt and pepper and dredge them lightly with flour, shaking off the excess.
2. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet and sauté the filets over high heat for 2 or 3 minutes on each side. Remove them carefully to a flameproof baking dish large enough to hold the fish in one layer.
3. In the same oil, sauté the shallots and 2 cloves of garlic until lightly browned.
4. Add the tomatoes and cook the mixture for 2 minutes.
5. Pour the mixture together with the herbs on top of the fish and cover the dish with buttered wax paper.
6. Bake in the oven at 350°F (175°C) for 25 to 30 minutes.
7. While the fish is baking, mash the avocado in a small mixing bowl, adding lime juice, salt, pepper, a little Tabasco, and the remaining clove of garlic. Do not mash it too fine; it should have some small chunks left in it.
8. When the fish is ready, lift it out carefully onto a platter.
9. Place the baking dish over direct heat and reduce the juices by about one-third.
10. If using Beurre Manie for thickening, add it gradually before adding the avocado puree.
11. Add the mashed avocado and correct the seasoning.
12. Once the avocado puree has been added, do not let the mixture come to a boil.
13. Pour the sauce over the fish.
14. Garnish with avocado slices, lime slices, and mint leaves.
